frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Nowadays some girls say no to everyone but later you find out they are already with someone. If she is committed, fine, I will step back out of respect for that man, but at least be clear instead of showing attitude. A decent family will not allow this confusion, brother or father should make sure such matters are handled properly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it treats a woman's rejection as less valid than her being attached to another man, and frames male relatives as the proper authority. It encodes the surface manifestations of only respecting a 'no' after learning she is 'taken' and using permission/ownership language through father/brother oversight.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
